macro_events.event_summary: Kevin Warsh, a known monetary hawk and longtime Fed critic favored by Trump, will be sworn in as Federal Reserve Chair on Friday.
primary_drivers: Hawkish regime shift at the Fed implying higher-for-longer real rates or at minimum less dovish reaction function
Regulatory rollback expectations for banks under a Warsh-led Fed (he has publicly favored lighter bank regulation)
Stronger USD / curve steepening expectations as Warsh has criticized QE and balance sheet expansion

beneficiaries.rationale: Warsh is ex-Goldman and has publicly advocated for reducing post-GFC bank regulatory burden and Basel III endgame rollback; large-cap banks with capital markets exposure benefit directly from lighter capital requirements freeing up buybacks.

Direct beneficiary of Basel III endgame softening and a steeper yield curve (higher NIM on a hawkish long end with eventual lower short end); largest US bank with most capital tied up by current SIFI surcharges.
Capital markets franchise benefits from regulatory rollback under a Warsh Fed plus revival of deal activity; Warsh's pro-markets posture historically associated with looser supervisory stance.

headlines: Kevin Warsh to be sworn in as Federal Reserve chair on Friday

macro_events.event_summary: A Wall Street commentator warns the oil market is approaching a "tipping point" that could spill over and pressure equities — an opinion piece without a specific catalyst or price move.
primary_drivers: Ambiguous directional thesis (tipping point could mean breakdown or breakout)
headlines: The oil market is reaching a ‘tipping point’ that could create problems for stocks, according to this Wall Street legend
